title: Snowflake Video Analytics with Snowpark & Cortex AI
code:
graph TD
    %% Ingestion
    Video[Raw Video]
    CV[Computer Vision Pipeline]
    JSON[Raw JSON Stream]

    %% Snowflake Data
    RawTable[(TRACKING_EVENTS - VARIANT)]

    %% Compute
    Snowpark[[Snowpark Metric Calc]]
    StatsTable[(MATCH_STATS - Structured)]

    %% AI
    Cortex[[Cortex AI Embeddings]]
    VectorIndex[(Vector Search Index)]

    %% App
    SiS[Streamlit in Snowflake]
    UserNode([User / Coach])

    %% Flow
    Video --> CV
    CV --> JSON
    JSON -->|Snowpipe| RawTable

    RawTable --> Snowpark
    Snowpark --> StatsTable

    StatsTable --> Cortex
    Cortex --> VectorIndex

    StatsTable --> SiS
    VectorIndex -.->|Semantic Search| SiS
    SiS --> UserNode

ai.summary: End-to-end data pipeline in Snowflake for video analytics, from raw video ingestion and computer vision to AI embeddings and user dashboards.
ai.whatItShows: This diagram illustrates a comprehensive data processing pipeline within the Snowflake Data Cloud. It details the flow from raw video ingestion and processing through a Computer Vision Pipeline, generating a raw JSON stream. This data is then ingested via Snowpipe into a Snowflake VARIANT table (TRACKING_EVENTS). Snowpark is used for metric calculations, populating a structured MATCH_STATS table. Cortex AI generates embeddings from this data, which are stored in a Vector Search Index. Finally, both the structured stats and the vector index are leveraged by a Streamlit in Snowflake application for a user/coach dashboard, enabling semantic search capabilities.
ai.whenToUse: Use this diagram to visualize data pipelines involving video processing, real-time ingestion, advanced analytics with Snowpark, AI/ML integration with Cortex AI for embeddings and vector search, and interactive dashboards built on Snowflake. It's suitable for demonstrating end-to-end data solutions in a cloud data warehouse environment.
ai.howToAdapt: This diagram can be adapted by changing the data source (e.g., IoT sensor data, log files), modifying the computer vision pipeline for different analytical needs, or integrating other Snowflake features like dynamic tables or external functions. The AI component can be swapped with different ML models or external AI services. The dashboard can be replaced with other BI tools or custom applications.
